meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master Claude Code: Accelerate Development for Students
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with slow development. Learn to integrate Claude Code and Antigravity to build high-quality software faster as a student developer.
content.answer_block: To overcome slow development, integrate Claude Code into your terminal to automate boilerplate and architectural scaffolding. By combining it with Antigravity for context-aware code generation, you reduce cognitive load, allowing you to focus on high-level logic rather than syntax, effectively doubling your production speed during your first major projects.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking with rhythmic indifference. You have the vision for your app, but the distance between the 'idea' and the 'working prototype' feels like a marathon run in lead boots. Every hour spent debugging trivial syntax errors is an hour stolen from your growth as an engineer.

But the industry is shifting. The most elite developers aren't just writing code; they are orchestrating AI agents to build it for them. By mastering Claude Code and Antigravity, you stop being a code-monkey and start acting as a Lead Architect. It is time to stop typing and start building.

steps.title: Initialize Environment with Claude
steps.description: Configure your project environment to allow Claude Code to index your existing architecture and project guidelines.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and create a comprehensive README.md that outlines the tech stack, directory purpose, and core business logic for this student project.

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Scaffolding
steps.description: Stop writing repetitive boilerplate. Use AI to generate foundational classes and interfaces based on your schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular CRUD controller and service layer based on the User model provided in the schema. Use industry-standard design patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate boilerplate for user authentication module'

steps.title: Contextual Bug Resolution
steps.description: Instead of Googling errors, feed the stack trace directly into the agent for immediate remediation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Debug this stack trace: [INSERT STACK TRACE]. Explain the root cause and provide a fix that adheres to our established architectural constraints.

steps.title: Refactoring for Performance
steps.description: Use Claude Code to review your code for optimization, ensuring your first project meets professional standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a code review on the main API route. Identify potential memory leaks or inefficient loops and refactor for production-grade performance.

steps.title: Documentation & Deployment Prep
steps.description: Automatically generate technical documentation and deployment scripts to finalize your project for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist and a setup script for this project so a recruiter can clone and run it in under two minutes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate setup.sh and update documentation'

pro_tips: Always use a .claudeignore file to keep the agent focused on your source code and away from heavy dependency folders.
Treat the agent as a junior developer; provide specific architectural constraints in your initial prompt to avoid 'generic' code output.
Use Claude's chain-of-thought capability by asking it to 'explain the logic' before it writes the implementation.
Keep your context window clean by periodically clearing terminal history if the agent begins to hallucinate previous session variables.
Map your project structure in a 'rules.md' file so the agent understands your preferred design patterns globally.
content.next_steps: After completing your first module, push your code to GitHub, write a brief 'lessons learned' post on LinkedIn, and start your next feature branch using the same workflow.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact workflow used by top-tier SaaS engineers to ship faster and learn deeper. Don't let your ideas stay in the planning phase. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
